label: Day 3 — Morning: Sustainable Lifestyles
description: We now zoom in from global awareness to personal lifestyle. This session makes sustainability tangible and individual: each participant calculates their own carbon footprint and compares it with others across different countries and contexts.
objectives: Calculate and understand individual carbon footprints
Compare lifestyle impacts across four different national contexts
Identify the highest-impact areas for personal change
Move from abstract statistics to personal, actionable insight

steps.title: What is a carbon footprint?
steps.body: Quick visual intro: what counts, what doesn't, how it's measured. Key stat: the average EU citizen produces 8.4 tonnes CO₂e per year. What does that actually mean?

steps.title: Personal Carbon Calculator
steps.body: Using the interactive calculator below, each participant calculates their own footprint across categories: food, transport, home energy, consumption, travel. Results are kept personal — this is not a competition.

steps.title: Country Comparison
steps.body: Teams share their category averages (not individual totals). Facilitator plots these on the live matrix below. Discussion: why are some categories so different across countries? What explains the gaps?

steps.title: My One Thing
steps.body: Each participant identifies the one category where they could most realistically reduce their footprint — and writes one specific action they could take within 30 days. Shared with their small group.

facilitatorNote: The carbon calculator can trigger guilt or defensiveness — especially when participants from lower-income countries see that their footprint is already much lower than their European peers. Address this proactively: the goal is not blame, but understanding. The structural vs individual responsibility tension is a rich discussion point.
